ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Dec-17-2019 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Dec-21-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Nancy Rasch for Richard Lambie (Administrator).
Stephanie Hess for and with Domenic Tallerico.

Ms. Rasch provided a copy of the declaration she filed on Friday addressing the Probate Examiner's notes, stated that she does not think an amendment is necessary for the statutory fees because there is a reduction, that the total fees do not exceed what was originally requested and that she does not see a purpose for an amendment. Ms. Rasch also stated that the reserve amount was reduced from $50,000.00 to $25,000.00, that Counsel for the Beneficiaries reviewed the declaration and the proposed order and have no objection, and that the only increase is what is going to be distributed. The Amended Petition for Final Distribution, etc. is under submission. Ms. Rasch provided a revised order.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Anasse. Not reported.

Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Jul-26-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Stephanie Hess for and with Domenico Tallerico.

Ms. Hess stated that the settlement agreement provides for a couple of contingencies and that they are hoping payment of the settlement amount can be made prior to distribution. The Court questions doing a preliminary distribution. Ms. Hess explains that it would be more of a creditor's claim and that Rosalie is not entitled to distribution. The Petition to Approve Settlement Agreement is under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel- Civil
Off Calendar Sep-13-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Joseph Piasta for Domenic Tallerico.

Mr. Piasta stated that Co-Counsel Heather Campopiano is not in the process of withdrawing, that Ms. Campopiano spoke with the Examiner, and that she told the Examiner that Mr. Tallerico is not opposing and that she will not be appearing today. Mr. Piasta also stated that he advised Mr. Tallerico yesterday by voice mail and e-mail about today's hearing. The Court granted the Motion to Be Relieved As Counsel (subject to review of the order). The Court noted that a petition for appointment of a general administrator has not been adjudicated.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Aug-15-2016 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Aug-15-2016 Continued To Nov-28-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Cecilia Fusich, for and with petitioner Rosemary Orlando (both by CourtCall); Heather Campopiano and Joseph Piasta, for and with Dominic Tallerico.

Mr. Piasta advised the Court that he had issues with the declaration by petitioner Rosemary Orlando, and he requested that the Court set an evidentiary hearing prior to ruling on the petition. Ms. Fusich responded that Ms. Tallerico wanted Ms. Orlando to be her administrator rather than her stepson Mr. Tallerico. The Court inquired regarding the number of projected witnesses and time for the proposed evidentiary hearing. Mr. Piasta replied that he expected it would be a five day hearing. He added that since the discovery of the death of Christina Tallerico, Dominic Tallerico has been doing all the heavy lifting for administration of the estate, including the payment of property taxes on all the estate properties and arranging for a haz mat team to clean up the home in which Mrs. Tallerico died (as her body was undiscovered until four weeks after her death). He suggested that pending the evidentiary hearing, Mr. Tallerico be appointed temporary administrator of the estate. Ms. Fusich responded that she did not believe a lot of discovery would be necessary prior to the conducting of an evidentiary hearing, and requested that the hearinf be set before the end of the year. Ms. Campopiano stated that so far Ms. Orlando has done nothing to protect the estate's assets, that there was no insurance on any of the proerties, and that Mr. Tallerico had obtained insurance on all of them. She further requested that the Court give authorication for Mr. Tallerico to engage the haz mat team to do the necessary cleanup. The Court granted the requested authorization, subject to review of order. He directed counsel to meet and confer regarding the filing of competing ex parte petitions for appointment of a temporary administrator, so they can be considered together. He further ordered the matter set for trial of the evidentiary hearing in Department 206 on November 28, 2016, at 9:30 am.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. (Not reported)
